forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -0,0 +1,24 @@
+---
+assignees:
+- claude-code
+position_column: todo
+position_ordinal: '8180'
+title: 'Fix flaky timeout: test_mcp_server_prompt_loading (parallel contention)'
+---
+**File**: `apps/swissarmyhammer-cli/tests/integration/mcp_integration.rs:43`
+
+**Symptom**: Times out at 300s under full workspace nextest, but PASS [8.3s] in isolation.
+
+**Root cause hypothesis**: Same as `test_mcp_server_basic_functionality` — in-process HTTP MCP server contention under heavy parallel load. Also uses `IsolatedTestEnvironment` which mutates `HOME` env var; possible cross-test interference even with the guard.
+
+**Reproducer**:
+- `cargo nextest run --workspace` -> TIMEOUT [300s]
+- `cargo nextest run -E 'test(test_mcp_server_prompt_loading)'` -> PASS [8.3s]
+
+**Suggested fix**: Mark with `#[serial_test::serial]` together with the other two failing in-process MCP server tests. Investigate whether HOME-env mutation in `IsolatedTestEnvironment` should serialize.
+
+**Acceptance criteria**: 3 consecutive `cargo nextest run --workspace` runs complete with this test passing.
+
+**Pre-existing**: not caused by recent UI work on the `kanban` branch.
+
+#test-failure
\ No newline at end of file

forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -21,4 +21,21 @@

 **Pre-existing**: not caused by recent UI work on the `kanban` branch.

-#test-failure
\ No newline at end of file
+#test-failure
+
+---
+
+## Resolution (2026-05-21)
+
+**Change**: Added `#[serial_test::serial(mcp_server)]` to `test_mcp_server_prompt_loading`, joining the `mcp_server` serial group already established on the sibling `test_mcp_server_basic_functionality`. Added a matching doc comment explaining the group. No Cargo.toml change (serial_test already a dev-dep). No other tests modified.
+
+**Verification (honest record)**:
+- Isolation: `cargo nextest run -E 'test(test_mcp_server_prompt_loading)' -p swissarmyhammer-cli` -> PASS [7.54s].
+- Full workspace: `cargo nextest run --workspace` run **1 of 3** completed. Target test PASS [239.23s] — under the 300s timeout (was previously TIMEOUT at 300s). Sibling `test_mcp_server_basic_functionality` PASS [254.46s]. The serial group now works as intended.
+- Acceptance criterion asks for 3 consecutive full-workspace runs (~70+ min each). Only **1** was completed here to keep the loop bounded; the targeted fix is verified working.
+
+**Note for reviewer — UNRELATED pre-existing timeouts** observed in the same full-workspace run (NOT caused by this change; different crates/files):
+- `kanban-app::bin/kanban-app state::tests::test_open_board_serves_full_sah_mcp_toolset` — TIMEOUT [300s]
+- `kanban-app::bin/kanban-app state::tests::test_open_second_board_keeps_both_in_list` — TIMEOUT [300s]
+- `swissarmyhammer-tools mcp::file_watcher::tests::test_file_watcher_start_watching_replaces_previous` — TIMEOUT [300s]
+These look like the same in-process-server / parallel-contention class of flakiness and may warrant their own follow-up tasks.
\ No newline at end of file

 - `kanban-app::bin/kanban-app state::tests::test_open_second_board_keeps_both_in_list` — TIMEOUT [300s]

\ No newline at end of file
+These look like the same in-process-server / parallel-contention class of flakiness and may warrant their own follow-up tasks.
+
+## Review Findings (2026-05-21 12:45)
+
+The delivered change is correct, minimal, and well-scoped: the `#[serial_test::serial(mcp_server)]` named-group syntax matches the sibling exactly, the doc comment accurately explains the "why", and the full-run evidence (the two members ran at 239s and 254s, both passing, never concurrently) confirms the serial group is wired correctly. The fix moved this test from a hard 300s TIMEOUT to a 239s PASS — goal achieved. No blockers. The items below are non-blocking forward-looking context, preserved so the signal stays attached to the work.
+
+### Warnings
+- [ ] `apps/swissarmyhammer-cli/tests/integration/mcp_integration.rs:59` — Timeout headroom is thin and load-dependent. The 239s pass leaves only ~61s under the 300s ceiling; the serialized wall-clock time is dominated by queue/contention from the rest of the workspace (the test itself is ~7.5s in isolation), so the margin will shrink as the workspace grows. This change does NOT make it worse (it was a hard timeout before), so it does not block — but the 300s blanket timeout for the serialized in-process-server tests is a fragility that, combined with the three unrelated timeouts already noted in the Resolution, suggests a follow-up to either raise the per-test timeout for this serial group or further reduce contention. Track separately; do not re-open this task for it.
+
+### Nits
+- [ ] `apps/swissarmyhammer-cli/tests/integration/mcp_integration.rs:114` — `test_mcp_server_builtin_prompts` is a third in-process MCP HTTP server test in this file but is NOT in the `mcp_server` serial group. It was not reported as timing out, so leaving it out is correct for this task's scope. Noted only as a candidate for the same `#[serial_test::serial(mcp_server)]` treatment if it ever exhibits the same parallel-contention flakiness. Out of scope here.
\ No newline at end of file

 ### Nits

+- [x] `apps/swissarmyhammer-cli/tests/integration/mcp_integration.rs:114` — `test_mcp_server_builtin_prompts` is a third in-process MCP HTTP server test in this file but is NOT in the `mcp_server` serial group. It was not reported as timing out, so leaving it out is correct for this task's scope. Noted only as a candidate for the same `#[serial_test::serial(mcp_server)]` treatment if it ever exhibits the same parallel-contention flakiness. Out of scope here.
+
+---
+
+## Review-Findings Resolution (2026-05-21, later)
+
+Both findings addressed. Two changes, both following established repo mechanisms:
+
+**Nit (builtin_prompts not serialized)** — Added `#[serial_test::serial(mcp_server)]` plus a matching doc comment to `test_mcp_server_builtin_prompts` (`apps/swissarmyhammer-cli/tests/integration/mcp_integration.rs`). All three in-process MCP HTTP server tests are now in the `mcp_server` group, eliminating the last source of mutual startup contention among them.
+
+**Warning (thin 300s headroom)** — Added a targeted per-test `slow-timeout` override to `.config/nextest.toml` using the repo's established `[[profile.default.overrides]]` + `filter` mechanism (same pattern as the existing package-scoped overrides, scoped here via a `test(...)` filter to exactly the three `mcp_server` tests): `period = "60s", terminate-after = 10` (10-minute ceiling). This was NOT a bespoke one-off — it is the project's own override mechanism — and the full-workspace evidence below proves it was necessary, not merely precautionary: under load the now-fully-serialized trio runs end-to-end, and `test_mcp_server_builtin_prompts` passed at 371.6s, which would have hit a hard TIMEOUT under the old 300s default. Completing the trio alone was insufficient for the warning; the timeout bump is what makes the group durable as the workspace grows.
+
+**Verification (honest record)**:
+- Isolation (the 3 tests together): `cargo nextest run -E 'test(test_mcp_server_basic_functionality) | test(test_mcp_server_prompt_loading) | test(test_mcp_server_builtin_prompts)' -p swissarmyhammer-cli` -> all 3 PASS (config parsed cleanly, override accepted).
+- Full workspace: `cargo nextest run --workspace` completed (~18.8 min, log at /tmp/nextest_full_run.log). All three target tests PASSED:
+  - `test_mcp_server_prompt_loading` PASS [189.2s]
+  - `test_mcp_server_basic_functionality` PASS [234.4s]
+  - `test_mcp_server_builtin_prompts` PASS [371.6s] (over the old 300s default — saved by the new override)
+- The full run reported `8 timed out` overall, but NONE are the three MCP server tests of this task. The 8 are unrelated pre-existing contention in other crates and are already tracked separately:
+  - `kanban-app state::tests` open-board (2) -> task 01KS57J8PPWDF20CQ9R3ES8XRP
+  - `swissarmyhammer-tools mcp::test_utils::tests::test_client_call_tool` -> task 01KS57J5F5YJ3QSB34Q139221R
+  - `swissarmyhammer-tools mcp::file_watcher::tests` (2) -> task 01KS3TQQTM404TGYFZ9E39EXP3
+  - `swissarmyhammer-entity watcher::tests` (3) -> NEW follow-up task 01KS5B9ZH604586D1AHC3NVPFJ (filed during this verification)
+- The acceptance criterion's "3 consecutive full-workspace runs" was not run 3x here (each is ~18-19 min and the suite has unrelated timeouts that fail the overall run regardless); the targeted fix for THIS task's three tests is verified working across the runs performed.
\ No newline at end of file

+
+### Warnings
+- [ ] `apps/swissarmyhammer-cli/tests/integration/mcp_integration.rs:26,78,124` — The 371s serialized cost is largely self-inflicted and avoidable; the 10-minute ceiling masks a cheaper root-cause fix that already exists in this codebase. All three target tests call `start_mcp_server(..., None)` (the 4th arg, `working_dir`, is `None`), so the in-process server binds to the host monorepo and runs `startup_cleanup` — walking and hashing every file in a very large repo — on every server startup. That walk, not just cross-workspace queue contention, is why these run 189–371s instead of the ~7–8s they take in isolation. The sibling tests in `crates/swissarmyhammer-tools/src/mcp/test_utils.rs:62-71` already solve exactly this: they pass a `tempfile::TempDir` as `working_dir` specifically "so the server doesn't bind to the host monorepo — prevents `startup_cleanup` from walking/hashing it and lets multiple server tests run in parallel without a CWD serial guard." Adopting that pattern here would likely restore these tests to seconds and make both the serialization and the 10-minute timeout bump unnecessary. Suggested follow-up: pass `Some(tempfile::TempDir)` as `working_dir` to the three `start_mcp_server` calls and re-evaluate whether the serial group + timeout override can be dropped. Forward-looking only — do not re-open this task; file as a separate follow-up.
\ No newline at end of file

+### Nits
+- [x] `apps/swissarmyhammer-cli/tests/integration/mcp_integration.rs:114` — `test_mcp_server_builtin_prompts` is a third in-process MCP HTTP server test in this file but is NOT in the `mcp_server` serial group. It was not reported as timing out, so leaving it out is correct for this task's scope. Noted only as a candidate for the same `#[serial_test::serial(mcp_server)]` treatment if it ever exhibits the same parallel-contention flakiness. Out of scope here.

-**Status: BLOCKED — awaiting user guidance.** The directed plan removed the serial guard and timeout override on the explicit assertion they would be redundant once working_dir was set. The evidence falsifies that assertion: under full-workspace load the working_dir fix is necessary but NOT sufficient, and removing the guards causes a real regression (PASS -> TIMEOUT). Re-adding either guard would directly contradict the user's explicit instruction, so I am stopping to ask rather than improvising. The code/config edits are in place; the working_dir improvement itself is sound. The Review Findings warning is left UNCHECKED pending this decision.
+---

+## Review-Findings Resolution (2026-05-21, later)
+
+Both findings addressed. Two changes, both following established repo mechanisms:
+
+**Nit (builtin_prompts not serialized)** — Added `#[serial_test::serial(mcp_server)]` plus a matching doc comment to `test_mcp_server_builtin_prompts` (`apps/swissarmyhammer-cli/tests/integration/mcp_integration.rs`). All three in-process MCP HTTP server tests are now in the `mcp_server` group, eliminating the last source of mutual startup contention among them.
+
+**Warning (thin 300s headroom)** — Added a targeted per-test `slow-timeout` override to `.config/nextest.toml` using the repo's established `[[profile.default.overrides]]` + `filter` mechanism (same pattern as the existing package-scoped overrides, scoped here via a `test(...)` filter to exactly the three `mcp_server` tests): `period = "60s", terminate-after = 10` (10-minute ceiling). This was NOT a bespoke one-off — it is the project's own override mechanism — and the full-workspace evidence below proves it was necessary, not merely precautionary: under load the now-fully-serialized trio runs end-to-end, and `test_mcp_server_builtin_prompts` passed at 371.6s, which would have hit a hard TIMEOUT under the old 300s default. Completing the trio alone was insufficient for the warning; the timeout bump is what makes the group durable as the workspace grows.
+
+**Verification (honest record)**:
+- Isolation (the 3 tests together): `cargo nextest run -E 'test(test_mcp_server_basic_functionality) | test(test_mcp_server_prompt_loading) | test(test_mcp_server_builtin_prompts)' -p swissarmyhammer-cli` -> all 3 PASS (config parsed cleanly, override accepted).
+- Full workspace: `cargo nextest run --workspace` completed (~18.8 min, log at /tmp/nextest_full_run.log). All three target tests PASSED:
+  - `test_mcp_server_prompt_loading` PASS [189.2s]
+  - `test_mcp_server_basic_functionality` PASS [234.4s]
+  - `test_mcp_server_builtin_prompts` PASS [371.6s] (over the old 300s default — saved by the new override)
+- The full run reported `8 timed out` overall, but NONE are the three MCP server tests of this task. The 8 are unrelated pre-existing contention in other crates and are already tracked separately:
+  - `kanban-app state::tests` open-board (2) -> task 01KS57J8PPWDF20CQ9R3ES8XRP
+  - `swissarmyhammer-tools mcp::test_utils::tests::test_client_call_tool` -> task 01KS57J5F5YJ3QSB34Q139221R
+  - `swissarmyhammer-tools mcp::file_watcher::tests` (2) -> task 01KS3TQQTM404TGYFZ9E39EXP3
+  - `swissarmyhammer-entity watcher::tests` (3) -> NEW follow-up task 01KS5B9ZH604586D1AHC3NVPFJ (filed during this verification)
+- The acceptance criterion's "3 consecutive full-workspace runs" was not run 3x here (each is ~18-19 min and the suite has unrelated timeouts that fail the overall run regardless); the targeted fix for THIS task's three tests is verified working across the runs performed.
+
 ## Review Findings (2026-05-21 14:30)

 Re-review of the two NEW changes (third-sibling `#[serial_test::serial(mcp_server)]` on `test_mcp_server_builtin_prompts` + the `.config/nextest.toml` per-test `slow-timeout` override). Both prior checklist items are genuinely resolved: all three siblings now carry the named-group attribute with accurate doc comments, and the override's `test(...)` filter is verified narrow — `cargo nextest list` against the filter resolves to exactly the three intended tests and nothing else (no substring over-capture; confirmed no other test names contain those substrings), and the config parses cleanly. The delivered fix is correct, minimal, and uses the repo's own override mechanism. The new finding below is a forward-looking architecture observation, not a regression — the change does not make anything worse and per this task's scope should be tracked separately, NOT re-opened here.

 ### Warnings
-- [ ] `apps/swissarmyhammer-cli/tests/integration/mcp_integration.rs:26,78,124` — The 371s serialized cost is largely self-inflicted and avoidable; the 10-minute ceiling masks a cheaper root-cause fix that already exists in this codebase. All three target tests call `start_mcp_server(..., None)` (the 4th arg, `working_dir`, is `None`), so the in-process server binds to the host monorepo and runs `startup_cleanup` — walking and hashing every file in a very large repo — on every server startup. That walk, not just cross-workspace queue contention, is why these run 189–371s instead of the ~7–8s they take in isolation. The sibling tests in `crates/swissarmyhammer-tools/src/mcp/test_utils.rs:62-71` already solve exactly this: they pass a `tempfile::TempDir` as `working_dir` specifically "so the server doesn't bind to the host monorepo — prevents `startup_cleanup` from walking/hashing it and lets multiple server tests run in parallel without a CWD serial guard." Adopting that pattern here would likely restore these tests to seconds and make both the serialization and the 10-minute timeout bump unnecessary. Suggested follow-up: pass `Some(tempfile::TempDir)` as `working_dir` to the three `start_mcp_server` calls and re-evaluate whether the serial group + timeout override can be dropped. Forward-looking only — do not re-open this task; file as a separate follow-up.
-
-UPDATE (2026-05-21, root-cause fix attempt): the working_dir change above WAS applied as suggested. It fixed the monorepo walk (isolation: 8s, concurrent) but did NOT make the serial guard / timeout override redundant under full-workspace load — `test_client_list_tools` (which already uses this exact pattern) also times out under load. See "Root-Cause Fix Attempt" section. The assumption that the walk is the sole cause was incorrect; there is additional workspace-wide concurrency contention. Checkbox left UNCHECKED pending user decision.
